vocab.andremo a: we will; we are going to
vocab.ci servirà: we will need; will be needed (to us)

vocab.sul bacino: on the pelvis; on the hips

advancedVocab.inserisco l'elastico: I put the band; I insert the elastic band (fitness context, resistance band)
advancedVocab.al di sopra: above; over (nuanced: physically above, not just 'on')
advancedVocab.creo tensione: I create tension (fitness context: make the band tight)
advancedVocab.in appoggio: supported; resting (nuanced: body part is supported by something)
advancedVocab.spingi sul bacino: push on the pelvis; push with the hips (fitness context)
advancedVocab.siamo già partite: we've already started; we're already underway (nuanced: feminine plural, group of women)

vocab.mi raccomando: please; make sure; I recommend (expression for emphasis or advice)
vocab.non mi avvicinate: do not bring close (you all); do not move close (imperative/plural)
vocab.sto avvicinando: I am bringing closer; I am moving closer

advancedVocab.mi raccomando: please; make sure; be careful (strong recommendation, often used to stress importance)
advancedVocab.abbiamo aggiunto l'elastico: we added the elastic band (refers to exercise equipment, may be unclear out of context)
advancedVocab.per tenerlo sempre in tensione: to keep it always under tension (specialized exercise instruction)
advancedVocab.non mi avvicinate le ginocchia: don't bring your knees closer (imperative form, may be confusing as 'mi' is reflexive here)

vocab.non è teso: is not tight; is not stretched

advancedVocab.divarica: spread apart; open wide (body part, e.g. legs)
advancedVocab.tieni sempre: always hold; always keep (imperative, continuous action)

vocab.vado a fare: I go to do; I am going to do

advancedVocab.Teso: tense; tight (can refer to body tension)
advancedVocab.stop: stop (used as an interjection, borrowed from English)
advancedVocab.Bridge: Bridge (refers to the 'bridge' exercise in fitness)
advancedVocab.div varico: I spread; I open (from 'divarico', fitness jargon for spreading legs)
advancedVocab.creo tensioni: I create tension (fitness context: muscle engagement)
advancedVocab.salgo col bacino: I lift my pelvis; I raise my hips (fitness context)

vocab.non far sì che: do not let; do not make sure that

advancedVocab.non far sì che: don't let; don't make sure that (nuanced construction meaning 'don't allow')
advancedVocab.rimango sempre qua: I always stay here; keep staying here (nuanced use of 'qua' for 'here', emphasizing position)
advancedVocab.rimani qua: stay here (imperative, used for exercise guidance)

vocab.ci portiamo: we bring ourselves; we move ourselves (reflexive construction)

beginnerVocab.scendi: go down; get down; come down

advancedVocab.crollare: to collapse; to crumble (can refer to physical or emotional collapse)
advancedVocab.Ci siamo quasi: We're almost there (idiomatic, used to indicate nearing completion)
advancedVocab.scendi qua: come down here; get down here (imperative, informal)
advancedVocab.ci portiamo in: we move into; we bring ourselves into (nuanced reflexive construction, can indicate positioning or transitioning)

advancedVocab.recuperiamo: we recover; we pick up (here: we grab/retrieve)
advancedVocab.sali giù: go up and down (literally: go up down; used for exercise movement)
advancedVocab.Io sto utilizzando: I am using (note: 'utilizzare' is more formal than 'usare')

advancedVocab.vai: go; start (imperative, informal, often used to begin an activity)

vocab.a terra: on the ground; to the ground

advancedVocab.recupero: I get back; I retrieve (here: 'I pick up again')
advancedVocab.inserisco sotto le ginocchia: I place/put under the knees (note: 'inserisco' is 'I insert', but here means 'I place')
advancedVocab.porto qua al centro: I bring here to the center (note: 'porto' is 'I bring', 'qua' is 'here', 'al centro' is 'to the center')

vocab.da un lato: on one side; from one side
vocab.sale troppo: goes up too much; rises too much

advancedVocab.all'elastico: to the elastic band; towards the elastic band (note: contraction of 'a' + 'il')
advancedVocab.piego: I bend (note: from 'piegare', can be confused with 'fold')
advancedVocab.da un lato: on one side; from one side
advancedVocab.Mi sale troppo: it goes up too much on me; it rides up too much (note: 'mi' is reflexive, indicating the elastic moves up on the speaker)
advancedVocab.spostarlo: move it; shift it (note: 'lo' refers to the elastic band)
advancedVocab.sempre un po': always a bit; just a bit (note: 'sempre' here means 'still' or 'just', not 'always')

advancedVocab.togliete tensione: release tension; loosen tension (note: 'togliere tensione' means to reduce or remove tension, often used with elastic bands or similar contexts)
advancedVocab.elastico: elastic band; resistance band (note: in fitness contexts, 'elastico' refers to a resistance band, not just any elastic object)
advancedVocab.mi va giù: goes down on me; slips down (note: 'andare giù' can mean to go down, but here it refers to the elastic band slipping or moving downward)
advancedVocab.ben teso: well-stretched; tightly stretched (note: 'ben' is a colloquial contraction of 'bene', meaning 'well', and 'teso' means 'stretched' or 'tense')

beginnerVocab.elastico: elastic band (note: can mean 'elastic', often exercise band)

advancedVocab.elastico sempre teso: band always taut; elastic always stretched

vocab.Vado a recuperare: I go to get; I go to retrieve

vocab.scendi bene: go down well; lower yourself properly

advancedVocab.scendi qua su squat: come down here into a squat (instructional phrase; 'squat' as exercise)
advancedVocab.Lascio i pesi: I put down the weights; I leave the weights (can mean 'stop using weights')
advancedVocab.a terra: on the floor; to the ground (can be misunderstood as 'earth')
advancedVocab.lavoro: I work; I exercise (here: 'I do the exercise')
advancedVocab.da un lato e poi dall'altro: on one side and then on the other (exercise instruction)
advancedVocab.posiziono l'elastico altezza caviglia: I place the band at ankle height (specific placement instruction)

vocab.torno giù: I go back down; I return down

advancedVocab.porto sua gamba tesa: I bring his/her/its leg straight (tesa = straight/tense; 'sua' can be confusing as it may refer to 'his', 'her', or 'its')
advancedVocab.torno giù: I go back down; I return down (giù = down; 'tornare giù' is a set phrase in movement contexts)
advancedVocab.salita del bacino: rise of the pelvis; lifting of the pelvis (salita = ascent/rise; used for movement)
advancedVocab.salita della gamba: rise of the leg; lifting of the leg

vocab.sono in appoggio: I am supported; I am resting
vocab.tengo fermo: I hold still; I keep steady
vocab.spingo bene: I push well; I push firmly
vocab.sul bacino: on the pelvis; on the hip area

advancedVocab.ultimo: last one; final (in this context, as a count)

advancedVocab.lato: side (can refer to body side or direction)
advancedVocab.mi posiziono: I position myself (reflexive, can mean 'I get into position')
advancedVocab.su col bacino: up with the pelvis (col = con il, colloquial contraction)

vocab.il linea: in line (note: likely 'in linea', meaning aligned)
vocab.martello: hammer (note: in dance/fitness, 'martello' refers to flexed foot)
vocab.vado a crollare: I go to collapse; I collapse
vocab.sono sempre qua: I am always here (note: means 'I always stay here/in this position')

advancedVocab.non vado a crollare: I don't collapse; I don't let myself fall (nuanced: not losing control or posture)
advancedVocab.non mi appoggio mai: I never lean; I never rest (nuanced: not putting weight down or relaxing completely)

vocab.andiamo a ripetere: let's repeat; we are going to repeat

beginnerVocab.a terra: on the ground; to the ground

advancedVocab.scendo: I'm going down; I descend (can be used as a workout instruction)
advancedVocab.recuperiamo: let's recover; let's get back (here: pick up or retrieve, not 'rest')
advancedVocab.Bridge: Bridge (exercise name; not a literal bridge)
advancedVocab.gambe leggermente di creo: legs slightly of I create (unclear/possibly a mishearing or typo, may confuse learners)

advancedVocab.tensione sull'elastico: tension on the elastic (exercise band tension)
advancedVocab.peso sul bacino: weight on the pelvis (body positioning in exercise)
advancedVocab.spingi su: push up (not the push-up exercise, but the action of pushing upward)
advancedVocab.Tieni qua: hold here; stay here (instruction to maintain a position)
advancedVocab.scendi lenta: go down slowly (feminine form, may confuse learners)
advancedVocab.Prova a: try to (imperative, often followed by a verb)

vocab.non avvicinare: don't bring closer; don't move closer

advancedVocab.seguire il mio ritmo: follow my pace; keep my rhythm (can refer to matching someone's speed or style, often in exercise or dance)
advancedVocab.scendi di nuovo su: come down again on (unusual phrasing; 'scendi su' is not standard, may mean 'lower yourself onto')
advancedVocab.tieni: hold; keep (imperative, can mean 'hold it', 'keep it', or 'maintain', depending on context)
advancedVocab.non avvicinare le ginocchia: don't bring your knees together (nuanced: 'avvicinare' means 'to bring closer', not just 'move')

vocab.sul bacino: on the pelvis; on the hips

advancedVocab.crollare coi glutei: collapse with your glutes; let your glutes drop (fitness context)
advancedVocab.setup elastico: elastic setup (using a resistance band or elastic equipment)
advancedVocab.tengo la: I hold the (incomplete, but 'tengo' as 'I hold' is less common than 'tengo' as 'I have')

vocab.vado a fare: I'm going to do; I go to do

advancedVocab.pse: PSE (abbreviation; context-specific, possibly 'pressione', 'posizione', or a technical term)
advancedVocab.non crollari: don't collapse; don't fall down (imperative, colloquial form of 'crollare')

advancedVocab.qua qua rimani qua: here here stay here (repetition for emphasis; idiomatic in instructions)
advancedVocab.gli ultimi secondi: the last seconds (set phrase; often used to encourage endurance)
advancedVocab.per concludere: to finish; to conclude (here: to wrap up an exercise)

vocab.round: round (as in a round of a game)

advancedVocab.round: round (as in a round of a game or competition; borrowed from English)
